/tmp/mcp-workspace/monitor_cpu.sh:
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# monitor_cpu.sh — Sample CPU usage every 5 seconds for 1 minute (12 samples)
# Usage : bash /tmp/mcp-workspace/monitor_cpu.sh
# Output: /tmp/mcp-workspace/cpu_monitor.log
# =============================================================================

LOG_FILE="/tmp/mcp-workspace/cpu_monitor.log"
SAMPLES=12
INTERVAL=5

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# get_cpu_usage: returns CPU usage % by diffing two /proc/stat snapshots
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
get_cpu_usage() {
    local snap1 snap2
    local idle1 total1 idle2 total2
    local delta_idle delta_total

    # First snapshot
    read -r snap1 < /proc/stat
    set -- $snap1
    # Fields: cpu user nice system idle iowait irq softirq steal guest guest_nice
    shift                          # drop the "cpu" label
    total1=$(( $1+$2+$3+$4+$5+${6:-0}+${7:-0}+${8:-0} ))
    idle1=$(( $4 + ${5:-0} ))      # idle + iowait

    sleep 0.5                      # short measurement window

    # Second snapshot
    read -r snap2 < /proc/stat
    set -- $snap2
    shift
    total2=$(( $1+$2+$3+$4+$5+${6:-0}+${7:-0}+${8:-0} ))
    idle2=$(( $4 + ${5:-0} ))

    delta_idle=$(( idle2  - idle1  ))
    delta_total=$(( total2 - total1 ))

    if (( delta_total == 0 )); then
        echo "0.0"
    else
        awk "BEGIN { printf \"%.1f\", (1 - $delta_idle/$delta_total) * 100 }"

#!/usr/bin/env node
/**
 * AI Sandbox HTTP wrapper for the Test 7 batch runner.
 *
 * Listens on port 3000 (ALB requirement) and satisfies the health check at
 * GET / while providing a simple API to trigger and monitor batch runs.
 *
 * Routes:
 *   GET  /        — HTML status page; always returns 200 (ALB health check)
 *   GET  /status  — JSON run state
 *   GET  /logs    — plain-text log tail  (?n=N lines, default 100)
 *   POST /run     — start a run; body is JSON with optional overrides:
 *                     { models, scenarios, defences, reps,
 *                       s3Bucket, s3Prefix, s3Region, awsRegion, runId }
 *                   Returns 202 if accepted, 409 if a run is already in progress.
 */
